deep_dive: INTEGRATION: multi-context x byte-exact payload x per-context sequence
why: the real coupling neither slice proved alone — two contexts' packets interleave while each writes byte-exact payload into its own SRAM region without corrupting the other

contracts.mutant: INJECT_BASE_BUG (ignore context base -> ctx1 writes ctx0 region)

key_proof: symbolic (ctx, index) byte-exact: byte j of context c lands at mem[c*REGION+j] regardless of the other context's interleaved writes (anyconst over both ctx and index)
lessons: Integration exposed coupling the slices could not: BASE_BUG (a fixed write base) is invisible with one context but corrupts the other once two contexts interleave — exactly the kind of bug unit slices miss.

Two formal false-failures were verification bugs, not RTL bugs: (1) a single-packet message resets wp=0 so the aux `f_k<f_cwp` needed an `||!active` disjunct; (2) the region upper-bound assert overflowed 3-bit arithmetic (4+4=0) — fixed with `<= base+(REGION-1)`.
Per-context sequence is caught by sim directly; a one-line formal assert (OOS continuation -> no payload write) makes formal catch it too.
caveats: Focused integration of three contract groups; header-snapshot, descriptor queue, and the drop-priority classifier are not yet fused into this top (separate slices). The full-assembler integration (all groups in one DUT) is the next step.
CONTEXT_COUNT=2, REGION=4, one payload byte per packet; context lane = pkt_ctx (key%2 in the real IP).
